text: Testing plays a vital role in software development. Unit tests check individual parts of the code, like functions or methods, while integration tests ensure that different components work together correctly. Many developers follow test-driven development (TDD), where they write tests before the actual code. This approach helps clarify requirements and makes code easier to maintain. Good testing gives developers confidence. When adding new features or fixing bugs, they can run the tests to quickly see if something breaks. Without testing, changes become risky and time-consuming. In short, well-designed tests save time, reduce errors, and make development more efficient and reliable.
